text: 0.7g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, the cleanest wins are low sugar and decent fibre.
11.9g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.
1.2g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, this really comes down to sugar control.
vitamin c at 31% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

the upsides first: polyphenol content adds extra upside. that gives it a useful antioxidant angle beyond the visible sugar and fibre story. contains useful polyphenols. adds plant-compound value beyond the base nutrient display. light for the volume. a practical advantage for a sweet food.

on the weaker side: quite perishable. storage life is not amazing compared with sturdier fruits. can be pricey. cost can limit how practical it is as a staple fruit. protein contribution is negligible. not a real flaw for fruit, but still basically absent.
S tier. Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
